arts.ttl: Elon Musk has expressed interest in buying English soccer club Liverpool, his father says
arts.sum: “Oh yes, but that doesn’t mean he’s buying it,” Errol Musk said. Liverpool, which leads by the Premier League and Champions League this season, did not have an immediate comment.

arts.ttl: Saudi-US brothers' £150m offer may be critical as Daniel Levy seeks Tottenham investment bidding war
Elon Musk would be interested in buying Liverpool FC, claims father
arts.sum: ‘He would like to yes, obviously. Anybody would want to’ Musk Sr says there is family connection to the city The father of the world’s richest man, Elon Musk, claims his son would be interested in buying Liverpool FC. Fenway Sports Group, which added the Reds to their sporting portfolio in 2010, has looked for external investment in the past but at no point has a full sale been seriously considered. Nevertheless, Errol Musk was asked whether his son had his eye on Anfield. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Spurs injury crisis persists ahead of ‘exciting’ Liverpool semi-final
arts.sum: Spurs boss Ange Postecoglou could be without 11 of his first-team players for the visit of Liverpool as the club’s injury crisis shows few signs of easing up. Tottenham Hotspur host the Reds in the first leg of their Carabao Cup semi-final on Wednesday night, with kickoff slated for 8pm. Guglielmo Vicario, Destiny Udogie, Ben […] The post Spurs injury crisis persists ahead of ‘exciting’ Liverpool semi-final first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Report: Tottenham have the edge over Juventus in race for 'one of the best strikers in Europe'
arts.sum: Tottenham Hotspur have hit the ground running in the January transfer window and it looks as though they’re only just getting started. Earlier this month,...

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola sends brutal Jack Grealish message as Tottenham transfer twist confirmed
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola has said that he is not seeing the best of Jack Grealish, and has urged him to fight for his place in the team, amid links with a move away from Manchester City in the January transfer window . The winger has fallen down the pecking order at the Etihad Stadium this season, with summer signing Savinho preferred by Guardiola in his starting lineup.

arts.ttl: Why Richarlison will not play for Tottenham vs Liverpool despite being spotted back in training
arts.sum: Tottenham boss Ange Postecoglou needs players to return to action and quickly. That has already happened this season, with Micky van de Ven and Cristian Romero both returning from injuries unexpectedly to face Chelsea on December 8, with both players failing to last the 90 minutes and not being seen in the squad since.

arts.ttl: football.london have a go at managing Tottenham in the January transfer window
arts.sum: With that in mind, we thought we'd have a go at managing Tottenham as football.london looks at the key decisions Spurs need to make in the January transfer window. Dominic Solanke needs help up front in the absence of Richarlison and a player like Randal Kolo Muani, who can play across the frontline, may just be what's required.
